entities.name: CROP YIELD
entities.description: The amount of crop produced per unit area.

entities.name: RAINFED ENVIRONMENTS
entities.description: Agricultural areas relying solely on rainfall for water.

entities.name: FOOD SECURITY
entities.description: The state of having reliable access to a sufficient quantity of affordable, nutritious food.

entities.name: CLIMATE CHANGE
entities.description: Long-term shifts in temperatures and weather patterns.

entities.name: DROUGHT
entities.description: A prolonged period of abnormally low rainfall leading to a water shortage.

entities.name: RESILIENT CROPS
entities.description: Crop varieties capable of withstanding adverse environmental conditions.

entities.name: AGRONOMIC PRACTICES
entities.description: Methods used in the management of crops and soil.

entities.name: HIGH-THROUGHPUT PLANT PHENOMICS
entities.description: Large-scale analysis of plant physical and biochemical traits.

entities.name: MODERN GENETIC APPROACHES
entities.description: Advanced techniques used to study and manipulate genes.

entities.name: ROOT DYNAMICS
entities.description: The growth and behavior of plant roots in response to the environment.

entities.name: TURGOR MANAGEMENT
entities.description: The process by which plants maintain internal water pressure.

entities.name: DESICCATION
entities.description: The state of extreme dryness, or the process of extreme drying.

entities.name: MOLECULAR RESPONSES
entities.description: Changes at the molecular level in response to a stimulus.

entities.name: DEHYDRATION
entities.description: The loss or lack of water.

entities.name: PLANT DEVELOPMENT
entities.description: The process of growth and differentiation of a plant.

entities.name: SEED ABORTION
entities.description: The failure of seeds to develop fully.

entities.name: GENETIC GAINS
entities.description: The increase in the genetic value of a population over time.

entities.name: CLIMATE-RESILIENT AGRONOMY
entities.description: Farming systems designed to withstand climate variability.

entities.name: ALLELES
entities.description: Alternative forms of a gene.

entities.name: DROUGHT TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of a plant to withstand drought conditions.

relationships.description: CROP YIELD is improved under RAINFED ENVIRONMENTS
CROP YIELD is key to meeting FOOD SECURITY
CLIMATE CHANGE causes expansion of DROUGHT
Used to understand factors controlling CROP YIELD
Used to understand factors controlling CROP YIELD
MOLECULAR RESPONSES occur in response to DEHYDRATION
